what_it_does: Defines a single BINDING dict named 'raw' for a radial view whose sectors are the distinct 'kind' values present in the store, with radius from time and slices ordered by count, plus a meta block of operator-facing name/is/fills/why text.
observations: BINDING fields: id='raw', label, angle_from='kind', radius_from='time', order_by='count'.
Described as 'THE DATA-DRIVEN DEFAULT' with no hardcoded sectors — sectors are the distinct kinds in the store.
meta provides four operator-facing strings (name, is, fills, why) explaining the view in plain language.
fills explains the radial encoding: each dot is one event, its slice is the kind, distance from centre is recency (centre = now).
Comments mark the copy as TENTATIVE draft for Tim/DNA ratification, journey-gated by OPERATOR-SURFACE-LOOP.md OQ1-4.
standouts: The data-driven default framing: sectors emerge from whatever kinds exist, no hardcode.
The explicit operator-law note that the operator never sees machine names.
conventions: Uses the same name/is/fills/why legibility field-set cited as composition's shape (ch-2mnxl9j0).
Marks human-meaning copy as TENTATIVE pending ratification.
Keeps the binding empty of meaning itself; the instrument reads and renders the meta.
notes: The default live-activity radial view binding: kinds as sectors, recency as radius, with plain-language operator copy.
questions: What is the full set of possible 'kind' values, and where are they enumerated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: declared as the data-driven default kinds view and that is exactly the binding it provides.
apparent_themes: Self-describing views that carry their own human meaning
intention_signals: Trying to give the operator an at-a-glance live map of system activity where structure (the sectors) comes entirely from the data rather than from configuration.
contribution: Contributes the default kind-by-time radial activity binding and its operator-facing copy describing the dot/slice/radius encoding.
summary_for_embedding: A Python binding defining the default live-activity radial view where sectors are the distinct event kinds in the store and radius encodes recency.
intention_for_embedding: To give the operator a glanceable, data-driven map of what kinds of things are happening and how recently.
